req.title: Account statement generation (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall request monthly statements as PDF; statements older than 7 years shall be fetched from cold storage. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
# Pytest Tests for Monthly Statement PDF Requirements

```python
"""
Test suite for monthly statement PDF generation requirements.
Covers:
- Happy path: statement retrieval as PDF
- Cold storage retrieval for statements older than 7 years
- Negative paths: insufficient funds, unauthorized access, exceeded limits
"""

import io
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch

import pytest
from dateutil.relativedelta import relativedelta

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Project-specific imports (adjust to your actual module structure)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from statements.exceptions import (
    ColdStorageUnavailableError,
    InsufficientFundsError,
    StatementLimitExceededError,
    StatementNotFoundError,
    UnauthorizedError,
)
from statements.models import Customer, Statement, StatementRequest
from statements.service import StatementService

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
COLD_STORAGE_THRESHOLD_YEARS = 7
VALID_CUSTOMER_ID = "CUST-001"
OTHER_CUSTOMER_ID = "CUST-002"
VALID_ACCOUNT_ID = "ACC-001"
PDF_MAGIC_BYTES = b"%PDF-"
MAX_STATEMENTS_PER_REQUEST = 12  # business limit: max 12 months per request


# ===========================================================================
# Fixtures
# ===========================================================================


@pytest.fixture
def valid_customer() -> Customer:
    return Customer(
        customer_id=VALID_CUSTOMER_ID,
        name="Jane Doe",
        email="jane.doe@example.com",
        account_ids=[VALID_ACCOUNT_ID],
        is_active=True,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def inactive_customer() -> Customer:
    return Customer(
        customer_id="CUST-INACTIVE",
        name="John Inactive",
        email="inactive@example.com",
        account_ids=["ACC-INACTIVE"],
        is_active=False,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def recent_statement() -> Statement:
    """Statement within the 7-year hot-storage window."""
    statement_date = datetime.utcnow() - relativedelta(years=2)
    return Statement(
        statement_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        customer_id=VALID_CUSTOMER_ID,
        account_id=VALID_ACCOUNT_ID,
        period_start=statement_date.replace(day=1),
        period_end=statement_date,
        created_at=statement_date,
        storage_tier="hot",
        balance=1_500.00,
        currency="USD",
    )


@pytest.fixture
def old_statement() -> Statement:
    """Statement older than 7 years – must be fetched from cold storage."""
    statement_date = datetime.utcnow() - relativedelta(years=8)
    return Statement(
        statement_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        customer_id=VALID_CUSTOMER_ID,
        account_id=VALID_ACCOUNT_ID,
        period_start=statement_date.replace(day=1),
        period_end=statement_date,
        created_at=statement_date,
        storage_tier="cold",
        balance=500.00,
        currency="USD",
    )


@pytest.fixture
def mock_hot_storage() -> MagicMock:
    storage = MagicMock()
    storage.fetch.return_value = PDF_MAGIC_BYTES + b" mock hot PDF content"
    return storage


@pytest.fixture
def mock_cold_storage() -> MagicMock:
    storage = MagicMock()
    storage.fetch.return_value = PDF_MAGIC_BYTES + b" mock cold PDF content"
    storage.is_available.return_value = True
    return storage


@pytest.fixture
def statement_service(
    mock_hot_storage: MagicMock,
    mock_cold_storage: MagicMock,
) -> StatementService:
    return StatementService(
        hot_storage=mock_hot_storage,
        cold_storage=mock_cold_storage,
        pdf_renderer=MagicMock(),
    )


# ===========================================================================
# Helper utilities
# ===========================================================================


def make_statement_request(
    customer_id: str = VALID_CUSTOMER_ID,
    account_id: str = VALID_ACCOUNT_ID,
    year: int | None = None,
    month: int | None = None,
    months: int = 1,
) -> StatementRequest:
    """Build a StatementRequest for a given period."""
    now = datetime.utcnow()
    target_year = year or now.year
    target_month = month or now.month
    return StatementRequest(
        customer_id=customer_id,
        account_id=account_id,
        year=target_year,
        month=target_month,
        months=months,
    )


def assert_valid_pdf(content: bytes) -> None:
    """Assert that content starts with the PDF magic bytes."""
    assert content.startswith(PDF_MAGIC_BYTES), (
        f"Response is not a valid PDF. First bytes: {content[:20]}"
    )


# ===========================================================================
# 1. Happy-path tests – recent statements (hot storage)
# ===========================================================================


class TestHappyPathRecentStatements:
    """Statements within the 7-year window are served from hot storage."""

    def test_request_single_monthly_statement_returns_pdf(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
        recent_statement: Statement,
        mock_hot_storage: MagicMock,
    ):
        """Customer can request a single monthly statement and receives a PDF."""
        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ), patch.object(
            statement_service, "_lookup_statement", return_value=recent_statement
        ):
            req = make_statement_request(year=2022, month=6)
            result = statement_service.get_statement_pdf(req)

        assert_valid_pdf(result.content)
        assert result.content_type == "application/pdf"
        assert result.filename.endswith(".pdf")

    def test_pdf_filename_contains_year_and_month(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
        recent_statement: Statement,
    ):
        """PDF filename must encode the statement period for auditability."""
        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ), patch.object(
            statement_service, "_lookup_statement", return_value=recent_statement
        ):
            req = make_statement_request(year=2022, month=6)
            result = statement_service.get_statement_pdf(req)

        assert "2022" in result.filename
        assert "06" in result.filename or "6" in result.filename

    def test_pdf_filename_contains_account_id(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
        recent_statement: Statement,
    ):
        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ), patch.object(
            statement_service, "_lookup_statement", return_value=recent_statement
        ):
            req = make_statement_request(year=2022, month=6)
            result = statement_service.get_statement_pdf(req)

        assert VALID_ACCOUNT_ID in result.filename

    def test_hot_storage_is_used_for_recent_statement(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
        recent_statement: Statement,
        mock_hot_storage: MagicMock,
        mock_cold_storage: MagicMock,
    ):
        """Verify hot storage, NOT cold storage, is called for recent data."""
        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ), patch.object(
            statement_service, "_lookup_statement", return_value=recent_statement
        ):
            statement_service.get_statement_pdf(make_statement_request())

        mock_hot_storage.fetch.assert_called_once()
        mock_cold_storage.fetch.assert_not_called()

    def test_statement_exactly_at_seven_year_boundary_uses_hot_storage(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
        mock_hot_storage: MagicMock,
        mock_cold_storage: MagicMock,
    ):
        """A statement exactly 7 years old (boundary) is still in hot storage."""
        boundary_date = datetime.utcnow() - relativedelta(years=7) + timedelta(days=1)
        boundary_statement = Statement(
            statement_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
            customer_id=VALID_CUSTOMER_ID,
            account_id=VALID_ACCOUNT_ID,
            period_start=boundary_date.replace(day=1),
            period_end=boundary_date,
            created_at=boundary_date,
            storage_tier="hot",
            balance=200.00,
            currency="USD",
        )
        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ), patch.object(
            statement_service, "_lookup_statement", return_value=boundary_statement
        ):
            result = statement_service.get_statement_pdf(make_statement_request())

        assert_valid_pdf(result.content)
        mock_cold_storage.fetch.assert_not_called()

    def test_request_returns_correct_account_statement(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
        recent_statement: Statement,
    ):
        """Returned statement belongs to the requested account."""
        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ), patch.object(
            statement_service, "_lookup_statement", return_value=recent_statement
        ):
            result = statement_service.get_statement_pdf(make_statement_request())

        assert result.account_id == VALID_ACCOUNT_ID


# ===========================================================================
# 2. Cold storage tests – statements older than 7 years
# ===========================================================================


class TestColdStorageRetrieval:
    """Statements older than 7 years must be fetched from cold storage."""

    def test_statement_older_than_seven_years_triggers_cold_storage(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
        old_statement: Statement,
        mock_hot_storage: MagicMock,
        mock_cold_storage: MagicMock,
    ):
        """Cold storage fetch is invoked; hot storage is NOT touched."""
        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ), patch.object(
            statement_service, "_lookup_statement", return_value=old_statement
        ):
            result = statement_service.get_statement_pdf(
                make_statement_request(year=2015, month=3)
            )

        assert_valid_pdf(result.content)
        mock_cold_storage.fetch.assert_called_once_with(old_statement.statement_id)
        mock_hot_storage.fetch.assert_not_called()

    def test_cold_storage_pdf_passes_pdf_validation(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
        old_statement: Statement,
    ):
        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ), patch.object(
            statement_service, "_lookup_statement", return_value=old_statement
        ):
            result = statement_service.get_statement_pdf(
                make_statement_request(year=2015, month=3)
            )

        assert_valid_pdf(result.content)
        assert result.content_type == "application/pdf"

    def test_statement_exactly_one_day_past_seven_years_uses_cold_storage(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
        mock_cold_storage: MagicMock,
        mock_hot_storage: MagicMock,
    ):
        """Edge case: one day past the 7-year threshold routes to cold storage."""
        cold_date = datetime.utcnow() - relativedelta(years=7) - timedelta(days=1)
        just_cold_statement = Statement(
            statement_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
            customer_id=VALID_CUSTOMER_ID,
            account_id=VALID_ACCOUNT_ID,
            period_start=cold_date.replace(day=1),
            period_end=cold_date,
            created_at=cold_date,
            storage_tier="cold",
            balance=100.00,
            currency="USD",
        )
        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ), patch.object(
            statement_service,
            "_lookup_statement",
            return_value=just_cold_statement,
        ):
            statement_service.get_statement_pdf(make_statement_request())

        mock_cold_storage.fetch.assert_called_once()
        mock_hot_storage.fetch.assert_not_called()

    def test_cold_storage_unavailable_raises_error(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
        old_statement: Statement,
        mock_cold_storage: MagicMock,
    ):
        """When cold storage is down a descriptive error must be raised."""
        mock_cold_storage.is_available.return_value = False
        mock_cold_storage.fetch.side_effect = ColdStorageUnavailableError(
            "Cold storage unreachable"
        )

        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ), patch.object(
            statement_service, "_lookup_statement", return_value=old_statement
        ):
            with pytest.raises(ColdStorageUnavailableError, match="Cold storage"):
                statement_service.get_statement_pdf(
                    make_statement_request(year=2015, month=3)
                )

    def test_cold_storage_response_is_logged_for_audit(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
        old_statement: Statement,
    ):
        """Cold storage retrievals must generate an audit log entry."""
        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ), patch.object(
            statement_service, "_lookup_statement", return_value=old_statement
        ), patch.object(statement_service, "_audit_log") as mock_audit:
            statement_service.get_statement_pdf(
                make_statement_request(year=2015, month=3)
            )

        mock_audit.assert_called_once()
        call_kwargs = mock_audit.call_args.kwargs
        assert call_kwargs.get("storage_tier") == "cold"
        assert call_kwargs.get("customer_id") == VALID_CUSTOMER_ID

    def test_cold_storage_statement_not_found_raises_error(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
        old_statement: Statement,
        mock_cold_storage: MagicMock,
    ):
        """A statement missing from cold storage raises StatementNotFoundError."""
        mock_cold_storage.fetch.side_effect = StatementNotFoundError(
            f"Statement {old_statement.statement_id} not in cold storage"
        )

        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ), patch.object(
            statement_service, "_lookup_statement", return_value=old_statement
        ):
            with pytest.raises(StatementNotFoundError):
                statement_service.get_statement_pdf(
                    make_statement_request(year=2015, month=3)
                )


# ===========================================================================
# 3. Negative path – Unauthorized access
# ===========================================================================


class TestUnauthorizedAccess:
    """Customers must only access their own statements."""

    def test_customer_cannot_request_another_customers_statement(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
    ):
        """A customer requesting another customer's statement gets UnauthorizedError."""
        intruder_request = make_statement_request(
            customer_id=OTHER_CUSTOMER_ID,
            account_id=VALID_ACCOUNT_ID,  # account belongs to CUST-001
        )
        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ):
            with pytest.raises(UnauthorizedError, match="not authorized"):
                statement_service.get_statement_pdf(intruder_request)

    def test_inactive_customer_cannot_request_statement(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        inactive_customer: Customer,
    ):
        """An inactive/suspended customer account must be rejected."""
        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=inactive_customer
        ):
            with pytest.raises(UnauthorizedError, match="inactive"):
                statement_service.get_statement_pdf(
                    make_statement_request(customer_id="CUST-INACTIVE")
                )

    def test_unknown_customer_id_raises_unauthorized(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
    ):
        """A completely unknown customer ID must not leak information – raises Unauthorized."""
        with patch.object(
            statement_service,
            "_get_customer",
            side_effect=UnauthorizedError("Customer not found"),
        ):
            with pytest.raises(UnauthorizedError):
                statement_service.get_statement_pdf(
                    make_statement_request(customer_id="CUST-GHOST")
                )

    def test_customer_cannot_access_account_not_in_their_portfolio(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
    ):
        """A customer requesting a valid-but-foreign account ID is rejected."""
        foreign_account_request = make_statement_request(
            customer_id=VALID_CUSTOMER_ID,
            account_id="ACC-BELONGS-TO-SOMEONE-ELSE",
        )
        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ):
            with pytest.raises(UnauthorizedError, match="account"):
                statement_service.get_statement_pdf(foreign_account_request)

    def test_missing_customer_id_raises_unauthorized(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
    ):
        """A request with an empty/null customer ID is rejected."""
        with pytest.raises((UnauthorizedError, ValueError)):
            statement_service.get_statement_pdf(
                make_statement_request(customer_id="")
            )

    def test_unauthorized_access_is_not_served_from_cache(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
        mock_hot_storage: MagicMock,
    ):
        """Even if a PDF is cached, an unauthorized requester must not receive it."""
        intruder_request = make_statement_request(customer_id=OTHER_CUSTOMER_ID)
        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ):
            with pytest.raises(UnauthorizedError):
                statement_service.get_statement_pdf(intruder_request)

        mock_hot_storage.fetch.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 4. Negative path – Insufficient funds / fee-gated statements
# ===========================================================================


class TestInsufficientFunds:
    """Some statement types require a fee; accounts with insufficient funds are rejected."""

    def test_account_with_zero_balance_cannot_request_paid_statement(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
    ):
        zero_balance_account = MagicMock()
        zero_balance_account.balance = 0.00
        zero_balance_account.statement_fee = 2.50

        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ), patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_account", return_value=zero_balance_account
        ):
            with pytest.raises(
                InsufficientFundsError, match="insufficient funds"
            ):
                statement_service.get_statement_pdf(make_statement_request())

    def test_account_balance_below_fee_raises_insufficient_funds(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
    ):
        low_balance_account = MagicMock()
        low_balance_account.balance = 1.00
        low_balance_account.statement_fee = 2.50  # fee > balance

        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ), patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_account", return_value=low_balance_account
        ):
            with pytest.raises(InsufficientFundsError):
                statement_service.get_statement_pdf(make_statement_request())

    def test_account_balance_exactly_equal_to_fee_is_accepted(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
        recent_statement: Statement,
    ):
        """Balance exactly covering the fee must succeed (boundary value)."""
        exact_balance_account = MagicMock()
        exact_balance_account.balance = 2.50
        exact_balance_account.statement_fee = 2.50

        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ), patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_account", return_value=exact_balance_account
        ), patch.object(
            statement_service, "_lookup_statement", return_value=recent_statement
        ):
            result = statement_service.get_statement_pdf(make_statement_request())

        assert_valid_pdf(result.content)

    def test_free_tier_account_is_not_blocked_by_funds_check(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
        recent_statement: Statement,
    ):
        """Accounts on the free tier (fee=0) must never hit InsufficientFundsError."""
        free_account = MagicMock()
        free_account.balance = 0.00
        free_account.statement_fee = 0.00  # free tier

        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ), patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_account", return_value=free_account
        ), patch.object(
            statement_service, "_lookup_statement", return_value=recent_statement
        ):
            result = statement_service.get_statement_pdf(make_statement_request())

        assert_valid_pdf(result.content)

    def test_insufficient_funds_error_does_not_deduct_fee(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
    ):
        """When funds are insufficient the fee must NOT be deducted."""
        account = MagicMock()
        account.balance = 0.50
        account.statement_fee = 2.50

        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ), patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_account", return_value=account
        ), patch.object(
            statement_service, "_deduct_fee"
        ) as mock_deduct:
            with pytest.raises(InsufficientFundsError):
                statement_service.get_statement_pdf(make_statement_request())

        mock_deduct.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 5. Negative path – Exceeded limits
# ===========================================================================


class TestExceededLimits:
    """Customers must not exceed the allowed number of statement requests."""

    def test_requesting_more_than_max_months_raises_limit_exceeded(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
    ):
        """Requesting more than MAX_STATEMENTS_PER_REQUEST months at once is rejected."""
        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ):
            with pytest.raises(StatementLimitExceededError, match="limit"):
                statement_service.get_statement_pdf(
                    make_statement_request(months=MAX_STATEMENTS_PER_REQUEST + 1)
                )

    def test_requesting_exactly_max_months_is_accepted(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
        recent_statement: Statement,
    ):
        """Requesting exactly MAX_STATEMENTS_PER_REQUEST months must not be rejected."""
        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ), patch.object(
            statement_service, "_lookup_statement", return_value=recent_statement
        ):
            result = statement_service.get_statement_pdf(
                make_statement_request(months=MAX_STATEMENTS_PER_REQUEST)
            )

        assert result is not None

    def test_daily_request_quota_exceeded_raises_limit_error(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
    ):
        """If a customer exceeds daily download quota the request is blocked."""
        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ), patch.object(
            statement_service,
            "_check_rate_limit",
            side_effect=StatementLimitExceededError("Daily download limit reached"),
        ):
            with pytest.raises(
                StatementLimitExceededError, match="Daily download limit"
            ):
                statement_service.get_statement_pdf(make_statement_request())

    def test_requesting_future_statement_raises_limit_exceeded(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
    ):
        """Requesting a statement for a future month must be rejected."""
        future = datetime.utcnow() + relativedelta(months=2)
        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ):
            with pytest.raises((StatementLimitExceededError, ValueError), match="future"):
                statement_service.get_statement_pdf(
                    make_statement_request(year=future.year, month=future.month)
                )

    def test_requesting_zero_months_raises_value_error(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
    ):
        """A request for 0 months is nonsensical and must be rejected early."""
        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ):
            with pytest.raises((ValueError, StatementLimitExceededError)):
                statement_service.get_statement_pdf(
                    make_statement_request(months=0)
                )

    def test_exceeded_limit_returns_informative_error_message(
        self,
        statement_service: StatementService,
        valid_customer: Customer,
    ):
        """The error raised for exceeded limits must include the allowed limit."""
        with patch.object(
            statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
        ):
            with pytest.raises(StatementLimitExceededError) as exc_info:
                statement_service.get_statement_pdf(
                    make_statement_request(months=MAX_STATEMENTS_PER_REQUEST + 5)
                )

        assert str(MAX_STATEMENTS_PER_REQUEST) in str(exc_info.value)


# ===========================================================================
# 6. Parameterized cross-cutting tests
# ===========================================================================


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "years_ago, expected_storage_tier",
    [
        (1, "hot"),
        (3, "hot"),
        (6, "hot"),
        (7, "cold"),   # exactly 7 years → cold (inclusive boundary)
        (8, "cold"),
        (10, "cold"),
        (15, "cold"),
    ],
)
def test_storage_tier_routing_by_age(
    years_ago: int,
    expected_storage_tier: str,
    statement_service: StatementService,
    valid_customer: Customer,
    mock_hot_storage: MagicMock,
    mock_cold_storage: MagicMock,
):
    """Parameterized check that storage tier routing is correct across ages."""
    statement_date = datetime.utcnow() - relativedelta(years=years_ago)
    statement = Statement(
        statement_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        customer_id=VALID_CUSTOMER_ID,
        account_id=VALID_ACCOUNT_ID,
        period_start=statement_date.replace(day=1),
        period_end=statement_date,
        created_at=statement_date,
        storage_tier=expected_storage_tier,
        balance=100.00,
        currency="USD",
    )

    with patch.object(
        statement_service, "_get_customer", return_value=valid_customer
    ), patch.object(
        statement_service, "_lookup_statement", return_value=statement
    ):
        statement_service.get_statement_pdf(make_statement_request())

    if expected_storage_tier == "hot":
        mock_hot_storage.fetch.assert_called_once()
        mock_cold_storage.fetch.assert_not_called()
    else:
        mock_cold_storage.fetch.assert_called_once()
        mock_hot_storage.fetch.assert_not_called()

    mock_hot_storage.fetch.reset_mock()
    mock_cold_storage.fetch.reset_mock()


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "customer_id, account_id, should_raise",
    [
        (VALID_CUSTOMER_ID, VALID_ACCOUNT_ID, False),          # valid
        (OTHER_CUSTOMER_ID, VALID_ACCOUNT_ID, True),           # wrong customer
        (VALID_CUSTOMER_ID, "ACC-FOREIGN", True),              # wrong account
        ("", VALID_ACCOUNT_ID, True),                          # empty customer
        (VALID_CUSTOMER_ID, "", True),                         # empty account
    ],
)
def test_authorization_matrix(
    customer_id: str,
    account_id: str,
    should_raise: bool,
    statement_service: StatementService,
    valid_customer: Customer,

    with patch.object(

"""
Test suite for monthly statement PDF generation requirements.
Covers:
- Happy path: statement retrieval as PDF
- Cold storage retrieval for statements older than 7 years
- Negative paths: insufficient funds, unauthorized access, exceeded limits
"""

import io
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch

import pytest
from dateutil.relativedelta import relativedelta

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Project-specific imports (adjust to your actual module structure)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from statements.exceptions import (
    ColdStorageUnavailableError,
    InsufficientFundsError,
    StatementLimitExceededError,
    StatementNotFoundError,
    UnauthorizedError,
)
from statements.models import Customer, Statement, StatementRequest
from statements.service import StatementService

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
COLD_STORAGE_THRESHOLD_YEARS = 7
VALID_CUSTOMER_ID = "CUST-001"
OTHER_CUSTOMER_ID = "CUST-002"
VALID_ACCOUNT_ID = "ACC-001"
PDF_MAGIC_BYTES = b"%PDF-"
MAX_STATEMENTS_PER_REQUEST = 12  # business limit: max 12 months per request


# ===========================================================================
# Fixtures
# ===========================================================================


@pytest.fixture
def valid_customer() -> Customer:
    return Customer(
        customer_id=VALID_CUSTOMER_ID,
        name="Jane Doe",
        email="jane.doe@example.com",
        account_ids=[VALID_ACCOUNT_ID],
        is_active=True,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def inactive_customer() -> Customer:
    return Customer(
        customer_id="CUST-INACTIVE",
        name="John Inactive",
        email="inactive@example.com",
        account_ids=["ACC-INACTIVE"],
        is_active=False,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def recent_statement() -> Statement:
    """Statement within the 7-year hot-storage window."""
    statement_date = datetime.utcnow() - relativedelta(years=2)
    return Statement(
        statement_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        customer_id=VALID_CUSTOMER_ID,
        account_id=VALID_ACCOUNT_ID,
        period_start=statement_date.replace(day=1),
        period_end=statement_date,
        created_at=statement_date,
        storage_tier="hot",
        balance=1_500.00,
        currency="USD",
    )


@pytest.fixture
def old_statement() -> Statement:
    """Statement older than 7 years – must be fetched from cold storage."""
    statement_date = datetime.utcnow() - relativedelta(years=8)
    return Statement(
        statement_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        customer_id=VALID_CUSTOMER_ID,
        account_id=VALID_ACCOUNT_ID,
        period_start=statement_date.replace(day=1),
        period_end=statement_date,
        created_at=statement_date,
        storage_tier="cold",
        balance=500.00,
        currency="USD",
    )


@pytest.fixture
def mock_hot_storage() -> MagicMock:
    storage = MagicMock()
    storage.fetch.return_value = PDF_MAGIC_BYTES + b" mock hot PDF content"
    return storage


@pytest.fixture
def mock_cold_storage() -> MagicMock:
    storage = MagicMock()
    storage.fetch.return_value = PDF_MAGIC_BYTES + b" mock cold PDF content"
    storage.is_available.return_value = True
    return storage


@pytest.fixture
def statement_service(
    mock_hot_storage: MagicMock,
    mock_cold_storage: MagicMock,
) -> StatementService:
    return StatementService(
        hot_storage=mock_hot_storage,
        cold_storage=mock_cold_storage,
        pdf_renderer=MagicMock(),
    )
